title: RIVER DISTRICT NAVIGATION CENTER - 211info
text: RIVER DISTRICT NAVIGATION CENTER RIVER DISTRICT NAVIGATION CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The Navigation Center is a temporary, low-barrier shelter serving up to 90 adults. The site has a space for coffee and tea, books and board games, a gated courtyard, showers and laundry, space to leave belongings during the day, and provides onsite meals. Staff will provide housing-focused support, employment and benefits assistance, and health-focused services. 1111 NW Naito Parkway Portland, OR 97209 Eligibility: Individuals age 18 and older experiencing homelessness, with priority for individuals age 55 and older, adults with disabilities, and veterans. Pets allowed; must be housebroken, and the owner is responsible for caring for, feeding, and cleaning up after the animal Hours: Shelter: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Resource Center phone hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm; service intakes end 15 minutes before close Resource Center walk-in hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm; service intakes end 15 minutes before close (650 NW Irving Street, Portland, OR 97209) (503) 280-4700 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (503) 280-4752 Main phone Main phone (503) 280-4700 INTAKE PROCEDURE: -Visit website to submit a Shelter Access Pre-Screening Form, or walk in or call the Resource Center (650 NW Irving Street) to sign up for a shelter bed -A Transition Projects Shelter Access Team member responds to all submissions to gather more information -Filling out a form does not guarantee a bed at a shelter -Reservations only and no intake at the shelter's physical location; without a reservation, people will be turned away -Once a space is reserved, the guest may continue to use the space until they no longer need it FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None LANGUAGES: English, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: Multnomah County TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Below the Broadway Bridge on the west side. Across the street from Albers Mill Building. Services: Community Shelters Community Shelters * Adults With Disabilities/Health Conditions Community Shelters * Homeless Veterans Community Shelters * Older Adults If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
